English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Presidents Council of Cornell Women records
President\'s Council of Cornell Women
Cornell University
41-9-3922
2.3 cubic feet. 2.3 cubic feet.
1990-2015. Computer media, photographs, research material. Includes meeting minutes, bylaws, membership information, publicity photographs, DVDs, CDs and videotapes. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 5 ======= 5 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 8601722
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
August 2014
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, August 2014
Date modified:
Jude Corina, February 2016
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
The President's Council of Cornell Women (PCCW) was founded in 1990 by former President Frank Rhodes. It is a group of alumnae working to enhance the involvement of women students, faculty, staff, and alumnae as leaders within Cornell University and its many communities.
Cite As:
President's Council of Cornell Women Records, #41-9-3922. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Includes meeting minutes, bylaws, membership information, publicity photographs, DVDs, CDs and videotapes.
Scope and content
PCCW Fall meeting material, 2014; PCCW Annual Meeting At-A-Glance program, 2014; 25th Anniversary Annual Meeting 2015 meeting packet, infographic poster signed by meeting attendees, and letter of congratulations from President David J. Skorton.
Videotapes. Photographs. Tompkins County (N.Y.) -- Social life and customs. Women college students. Women -- New York (State) -- Ithaca -- Societies and clubs. Cornell University -- : Alumni and alumnae. Cornell University -- : Students. Cornell University -- : Faculty.
